---
title: "Creating A Class"
---

To classify documents, each document that is uploaded into the system needs to be assigned to a document class. Document classes group together documents with similar characteristics. Each document class has certain fields associated with it by default.

- The Class Suggestions area displays a list of suggested classes for each document only if the Class Suggestions option is enabled in Settings. To enable or disable the class suggestions, click the ellipsis button and toggle the Class Suggestions option. ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
- Click the Refresh icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) to refresh the list of suggested classes.
- Click Accept All to accept all class suggestions.
- Click Reject All to reject all class suggestions.

You can configure which fields are associated with a document class when you create a new document class or edit an existing document class. For more information on defining fields, see Creating a Field.

To create a document class:

1.  On the Class page, click + Create Class. The Add Class dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  Enter a name for the document class in the Name field. You may enter a Description for the document class.  Note:
- The document class name must be unique.
- To create another class, click Create another.
3.  Optional. Click the Options tab to configure class properties. For more information on class properties, see Manage Class Properties.
4.  Click Save. The class is created and is displayed in the left navigation pane.  Note: You can assign a color badge to each class. To change the default color, click the colored dot and select an appropriate color in the Select a color box.  ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Click Classify Documents. The documents are classified by the AI-enabled classification engine under the respective classes created by you.  Note:
- You can use the Document Separation feature to separate and split documents that are uploaded as one single file into individual documents. To enable document separation, click the ellipsis button next to Build Process and enable the Document Separation option. You will need to create all classes corresponding to the uploaded document bundle, before using this feature.
- Click the Info icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) next to the document name to view the document classification candidates. You can also view the confidence percentage and reasoning for each candidate.
- To delete the selected document, click the ellipsis button and select Delete Document.
- To classify the selected document, click the ellipsis button and select Classify Document.
![](data:image/gif;base64,)
6.  Click the relevant class to view the corresponding AI generated field suggestions for the selected class.
AI generated field suggestions are prompted only if the AI Field Suggestions option is enabled. For more information on configuring field extraction, see Creating a Field.

Note:
- To create a child class, click the + icon next to the parent class name. For more information, refer to the Creating a Parent Class topic.
- To modify the name of a class, select the relevant class and click the Edit class ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon.
- To delete a class, select the relevant class and click the Delete class ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon.
- To upload more documents, click Upload Files.
